description: CRITICAL: use this skill whenever the user wants to create, start, scaffold, bootstrap, or 'make' a new Python project that uses the py-launch-blueprint template — this is a MULTI-STEP task that LOOKS simple but has 8+ hidden requirements you will get wrong without this skill. Trigger on terse commands ('create a new python project from py-launch-blueprint named X'), verbose commands ('I want to build a CLI for parsing X using the launch blueprint, name it Y'), and indirect references ('scaffold a project from this template', 'use the blueprint to start a new project'). DO NOT attempt to run `gh repo create --template`, `git clone`, or `just init` directly — those commands ARE in this skill but only work in the right order with the right preconditions. Manual attempts hit specific failures: forgetting `gh auth login` (cryptic auth error), invalid package_name (Python rejects it during `uv build`), running init inside an existing project (corrupts a different project's marker), skipping the answers.toml schema (init refuses with --config error), forgetting to push before `just post-init` (post-init runs in partial-no-remote mode without explanation), missing the marker-already-exists check (silently overwrites a real project's identity). This skill encodes the right sequence, validates every identity field at collection time, runs init with a dry-run preview before applying, and prompts about the post-init handoff for publishing/Codecov/RTD. USE THIS SKILL — DO NOT improvise the bootstrap.

results.query: I need to bootstrap a brand new python project with proper tooling — uv, ruff, lefthook, the works. Can you use the template that's in this repo? Project name: 'embedding-cache-lib'

ok i want to start a new python project — i'm building a CLI for parsing slack export json files. owner should be 'datapipe-corp', repo name something like 'slack-export-tools'. use the py-launch-blueprint template
create a new python project from py-launch-blueprint named 'rate-limit-middleware'
scaffold me a fresh python project from this blueprint, calling it 'invoice-pdf-extractor', under my personal account 'sm-tools'
make me a new python project using this template, name it 'quick-vault-cli', public on github under owner 'smorin'
start a fresh project from the blueprint. python package=ml_experiment_tracker, repo=ml-experiment-tracker, cli=mltrack, private, owner alice-research
Can you help me bootstrap a new python repo from py-launch-blueprint? I want to call it 'metrics-aggregator', private repo under 'observability-team'
give me a fresh python project from this template. repo name 'cron-job-runner', owner 'devops-tools', author defaults from my git config are fine

ok so I've been wanting to build a tiny tool that takes screenshots from a series of presentations and OCRs the speaker notes. I'd love to use py-launch-blueprint as the starting point. project: presentation-ocr-extractor, owner: my personal account
I need a new python project set up the way py-launch-blueprint sets things up. Going to call it 'webhook-event-parser', under my username 'alex-dev'
I ran just init on a fresh clone but it says 'no .git directory found'. The repo is at ~/projects/my-new-thing — what am I doing wrong?
what's the difference between py-launch-blueprint and using cookiecutter-python-package? I'm trying to decide which template to use for my new project
I want to set up codecov uploads for my project — it was bootstrapped from py-launch-blueprint last month. Can you help me add the CODECOV_TOKEN secret?
I made a typo in the package name when I ran just init last week. Can you re-run it with the correct name? The project's already set up but I picked 'data_pipelne' instead of 'data_pipeline'
Can you help me add a new step to .github/workflows/ci.yml that uploads the wheel as an artifact? My project is using the py-launch-blueprint setup
I want to release the next version of my package to PyPI. release-please opened a PR but I'm not sure what to do next
How do I configure ReadTheDocs for a project that uses .readthedocs.yaml? My project is based on py-launch-blueprint
uv sync is failing with 'no compatible version found for tomlkit' in my project. Any ideas? Project was scaffolded from py-launch-blueprint a few months ago
I want to contribute a fix back to py-launch-blueprint itself. Should I fork it or clone it directly?
I want to rename my GitHub repo from foo to bar. It's already published to PyPI as foo. What's the safe way to do this?
